books.title: The Name of the Wind
books.author: Patrick Rothfuss
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A legendary figure recounts his own life story to a chronicler, from poverty and magic school to infamy. Near-universal praise for the prose — main caveat is Book 3 has been unreleased for over a decade.

books.title: Mistborn
books.author: Brandon Sanderson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A street thief discovers rare magical abilities in a world ruled by an immortal tyrant, and joins a heist crew to overthrow him. Praised for inventive, rule-based magic and tight plotting.

books.title: The Way of Kings
books.author: Brandon Sanderson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: On the war-torn world of Roshar, a soldier, a scholar, and a young thief are drawn into an ancient conflict against forces that defy comprehension. The first of five planned books — each is a brick, but the payoff is enormous.

books.title: Words of Radiance
books.author: Brandon Sanderson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: The second Stormlight Archive book escalates the stakes dramatically — major revelations about the Radiants, the Parshendi, and the Desolations. Generally considered even better than Book 1.

books.title: Oathbringer
books.author: Brandon Sanderson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: The third book shifts focus to Dalinar and the history of the Knights Radiant. Praised for its emotional weight and worldbuilding depth; the size (1,200+ pages) is daunting but rewarding.

books.title: Rhythm of War
books.author: Brandon Sanderson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: The fourth book focuses heavily on Eshonai and Venli's backstory and the war on Shadesmar. Divisive on pacing mid-book but widely praised for its ending and character arcs.

books.title: The Lies of Locke Lamora
books.author: Scott Lynch
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A con artist and his crew navigate the criminal underworld of a Venice-like fantasy city. Critically loved for its wit and heist-style plotting.

books.title: A Wizard of Earthsea
books.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A boy wizard's coming-of-age story, more about balance and consequence than power fantasy. A genre-defining classic; some modern readers find the pacing slow.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.label: safest
books.blurb: The foundational starting point for The Lord of the Rings — the source of most tropes other fantasy borrows from. Start here before Fellowship. Near-universal reverence as the genre's cornerstone.

books.title: Jade City
books.author: Fonda Lee
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A crime-family epic in an Asian-inspired city where magical jade grants supernatural abilities. Award-winning, praised for its fresh setting and political intrigue.

books.title: The Witcher: The Last Wish
books.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A monster hunter for hire navigates a morally grey world — the source material for the games and show. The early short-story collections are the ideal entry point. Well-regarded for their cynical tone.

books.title: Trail of Lightning
books.author: Rebecca Roanhorse
books.label: safest
books.blurb: Post-apocalyptic fantasy rooted in Navajo mythology, following a monster-hunter heroine. Praised for its unique cultural foundation and fresh urban fantasy take.

books.title: Gardens of the Moon
books.author: Steven Erikson
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A genre-defining epic with an enormous cast and a 'drop you in the deep end' approach to worldbuilding. Beloved by die-hard fans for scope and ambition — notorious for confusing first-time readers.

books.title: The Blade Itself
books.author: Joe Abercrombie
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A grimdark trilogy that deliberately subverts classic hero tropes with morally grey, often unlikeable characters. Loved for dark humor and trope subversion; criticized by some for being bleak by design.

books.title: Jonathan Strange & Mr Norrell
books.author: Susanna Clarke
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A literary, alternate-history take on magic's return to 19th-century England. Critically acclaimed — some readers find the pacing slow and the prose deliberately dense and old-fashioned.

books.title: The Dark Tower: The Gunslinger
books.author: Stephen King
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A gunslinger's quest across a decaying multiverse blending fantasy, western, and horror. Deeply loved for ambition and worldbuilding; the ending of the series is famously divisive.

books.title: Vicious
books.author: V.E. Schwab
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: Two former friends turned rivals after gaining superpowers through near-death experiments. Well-liked and fast-paced with morally grey characters; some find the out-of-order structure gimmicky.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
books.label: adjacent
books.blurb: A human envoy navigates an alien world where inhabitants have no fixed gender. Hugely respected, award-winning, and ahead of its time. More cerebral than plot-driven.

books.title: All Systems Red
books.author: Martha Wells
books.label: adjacent
books.blurb: A security android who'd rather watch TV than deal with humans gets dragged into adventures anyway. Extremely well-loved, witty, and fast reads — start with the first novella.

books.title: The Neverending Story
books.author: Michael Ende
books.label: adjacent
books.blurb: A boy is pulled into a magical book and becomes part of the story he's reading. The full novel is darker and deeper than the well-known film adaptation.

books.title: Final Girls
books.author: Riley Sager
books.label: safest
books.blurb: Twisty psychological thriller with surprise reveals — Sager's signature style. Generally well-enjoyed and fast-paced. Some readers note his twists feel formulaic once you've read several of his books.

books.title: The House Across the Lake
books.author: Riley Sager
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A widow gets drawn into a dangerous situation involving her glamorous neighbor. Considered one of Sager's stronger entries.

books.title: Behind Closed Doors
books.author: B.A. Paris
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A seemingly perfect marriage hides a controlling, abusive truth. Well-reviewed and tense; some find it bleak rather than 'fun' to read.

books.title: The Hunting Party
books.author: Lucy Foley
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A group of friends at a remote Scottish lodge for New Year's — one of them turns up dead. A solid, well-executed whodunit.

books.title: Still Missing
books.author: Chevy Stevens
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A realtor is abducted and held captive for nearly a year, then must rebuild her life after escaping. Strong reviews for psychological depth; intense and dark for some readers.

books.title: Misery
books.author: Stephen King
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A famous author is held captive by an obsessive fan. A horror-thriller classic with near-universal praise.

books.title: Gone Girl
books.author: Gillian Flynn
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A husband becomes the prime suspect when his wife disappears, in one of the genre's most famous unreliable-narrator twists. Critically acclaimed and hugely influential.

books.title: Everyone in My Family Has Killed Someone
books.author: Benjamin Stevenson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A murder mystery with a comedic, self-aware tone — the narrator openly references mystery tropes as he tells the story. Very well-loved, including its recently released sequel.

books.title: The Woman in the Window
books.author: A.J. Finn
books.label: safest
books.blurb: An agoraphobic woman witnesses a crime from her window and isn't sure anyone will believe her. Popular and fast; some find it derivative of Rear Window.

books.title: Rock, Paper, Scissors
books.author: Alice Feeney
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A couple on a remote anniversary trip uncovers unsettling secrets about their marriage. Well-liked and twisty.

books.title: Sometimes I Lie
books.author: Alice Feeney
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A woman wakes up in a hospital, unable to move or speak, recalling the events leading to her accident. Well-reviewed psychological thriller.

books.title: The Kind Worth Killing
books.author: Peter Swanson
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A chance airport conversation leads two strangers to plot a murder together. Well-regarded, twisty and dark — considered some of the stronger 'stranger conspiracy' thrillers in the genre.

books.title: Verity
books.author: Colleen Hoover
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A struggling writer is hired to finish a bestselling author's series and uncovers dark secrets in her manuscripts. Loved for being unputdownable; criticized for a messy ending and uneven tone between romance and thriller.

books.title: In the Woods
books.author: Tana French
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A detective investigates a child's murder that echoes an unsolved disappearance from his own childhood. Loved for prose and atmosphere; the central mystery is deliberately left unresolved — which frustrates many readers.

books.title: Behind Her Eyes
books.author: Sarah Pinborough
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A woman gets entangled with a married couple, leading to a famously wild twist. Extremely divisive — fans call the twist unforgettable, critics say it veers into territory that feels like cheating.

books.title: The Last House on Needless Street
books.author: Catriona Ward
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A reclusive man, his daughter, and a cat in a house at the edge of the woods — slow-burn horror-thriller hybrid. Critically praised and award-winning; deliberately disorienting structure frustrates some readers.

books.title: Hatchet
books.author: Gary Paulsen
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A 13-year-old boy survives alone in the Canadian wilderness after a plane crash, armed with only a hatchet. A beloved classic — near-universal praise, often a formative read but holds up well for adults.

books.title: The Nazi and the Psychiatrist
books.author: Jack El-Hai
books.label: safest
books.blurb: True story of the U.S. Army psychiatrist assigned to evaluate Hermann Göring and other Nazi leaders at Nuremberg — and how studying evil up close unraveled his own life. NYT bestseller; recently adapted into the film Nuremberg.

books.title: 22 Cells in Nuremberg
books.author: Douglas M. Kelley
books.label: safest
books.blurb: The firsthand account by the actual psychiatrist who evaluated the 22 top Nazi leaders at Nuremberg. A rare, historically significant primary source — praised as one of the most important books published on WWII.

books.title: A Man Called Ove
books.author: Fredrik Backman
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A grumpy, routine-obsessed widower's life is upended by an unlikely group of neighbors. Widely beloved, heartwarming without being saccharine — near-universal praise as a feel-good modern classic.

books.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
books.author: Rachel Joyce
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A retired man sets off on an impromptu, life-changing walk across England to visit a dying former friend. Gentle, quirky, well-loved bestseller.

books.title: The Poisonwood Bible
books.author: Barbara Kingsolver
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A missionary family's life unravels in the Congo, narrated by the wife and four daughters across decades. Critically acclaimed, often called a modern masterpiece (Pulitzer finalist).

books.title: Animal Dreams
books.author: Barbara Kingsolver
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A woman returns to her Arizona hometown to care for her ailing father and confronts her past and the town's environmental crisis. Well-regarded literary fiction; praised for grounded characters.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A young girl in Depression-era Alabama watches her father defend a Black man falsely accused of rape. Considered one of the greatest American novels and a staple of school curricula.

books.title: Jock of the Bushveld
books.author: Percy FitzPatrick
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A memoir of adventures with a loyal hunting dog in turn-of-the-century South African bushveld. A beloved South African classic, consistently well-reviewed by those who've read it.

books.title: Scattered Minds
books.author: Gabor Maté
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A trauma-informed look at ADHD, arguing it stems from early childhood environment rather than purely genetics. Respected in trauma and psychology circles; controversial among some clinicians.

books.title: The Art of Hearing Heartbeats
books.author: Jan-Philipp Sendker
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A New York lawyer travels to Burma to uncover her father's hidden past and a decades-old love story. Loved by many for its romantic, atmospheric storytelling; some find it overly sentimental.

books.title: Eisenhorn: Xenos
books.author: Dan Abnett
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A detective/thriller story following an Imperial Inquisitor hunting a sinister conspiracy. Widely considered the best 40k entry point — requires zero prior lore knowledge.

books.title: Gaunt's Ghosts: First and Only
books.author: Dan Abnett
books.label: safest
books.blurb: 'Band of Brothers in space' — follows a regiment of Imperial Guard soldiers through brutal warfare. A beloved series for its grounded, human-scale perspective on the setting.

books.title: Helsreach
books.author: Aaron Dembski-Bowden
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A single desperate last-stand battle as Space Marines defend a hive city from an Ork invasion. Praised as the purest distillation of 40k's tone — a great standalone taste test.

books.title: Horus Rising
books.author: Dan Abnett
books.label: commitment
books.blurb: The start of the 90+ book Horus Heresy saga, chronicling the betrayal that defines the entire 40k timeline. A strong start, but the full series is a serious long-term commitment.

books.title: Man's Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor Frankl
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A psychiatrist's account of surviving Nazi concentration camps and the philosophy of meaning he developed from it. A widely respected modern classic with little real contention.

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.label: safest
books.blurb: Personal philosophical journal of a Roman emperor practicing Stoicism. A foundational text. Some find Stoic philosophy repetitive or emotionally cold.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A practical framework for building good habits through small incremental changes. Popular and practical; common criticism is that it repackages common sense.

books.title: The War of Art
books.author: Steven Pressfield
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A short, punchy book on overcoming 'Resistance' — the internal force that stops people from doing creative work. Loved by creatives for being concise and motivating.

books.title: Born a Crime
books.author: Trevor Noah
books.label: safest
books.blurb: Memoir of growing up mixed-race in apartheid South Africa, blending humor with hard truths. Very well-loved — the audiobook narrated by Noah himself is often recommended as even better than the text.

books.title: Into the Wild
books.author: Jon Krakauer
books.label: safest
books.blurb: True story of a young man who abandoned his possessions to live in the Alaskan wilderness, and died there. Gripping and widely respected; debate exists over whether the subject was inspiring or reckless.

books.title: Catch-22
books.author: Joseph Heller
books.label: safest
books.blurb: A satirical anti-war novel about the absurd bureaucratic logic soldiers face in WWII. A widely respected classic of dark satire.

books.title: Fight Club
books.author: Chuck Palahniuk
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A disaffected office worker forms an underground fight club that spirals into anarchist chaos. A cult classic; some find the philosophy and its real-world misreadings genuinely concerning rather than just edgy.

books.title: Never Finished
books.author: David Goggins
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: The follow-up to Can't Hurt Me, going deeper into Goggins's mindset on self-discipline and pushing past perceived limits. Fans enjoy the same intensity; critics say his extreme approach isn't realistic for most people.

books.title: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
books.author: Hunter S. Thompson
books.label: polarizing
books.blurb: A drug-fueled, semi-fictionalized account of a wild trip to Vegas — a landmark of gonzo journalism. A cult favorite; some find the glorification of drug use off-putting rather than fun.
